title: spathodea campanulata subsp. congolana
intro: Spathodea campanulata subsp. congolana, also known as the African tulip tree or Congo tulip tree, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities, derived from its leaves, bark, and roots. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory issues, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, Spathodea campanulata subsp. congolana is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical paste, and is also used in herbal remedies such as teas, tonics, and ointments.
